title: centunculus pulcherrimus
intro: Centunculus pulcherrimus, also known as sweet woodruff, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant's medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its essential oils, which contain linalool and linalyl acetate, and have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of sweet woodruff include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve anxiety and stress, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. Sweet woodruff can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried herb, and is often used in traditional medicine and culinary applications.
